daily_summary.headline: Aurora Storm and Multiple Launches Highlight the Day

top_events.title: Aurora Storm Predicted
top_events.description: A geomagnetic storm with a Kp index of 6.33 is forecasted, offering a great chance to view auroras at latitudes as low as 55°.
top_events.action: Prepare for aurora viewing if you're in the visibility range.

top_events.title: Falcon 9 Starlink Launches
top_events.description: Multiple Falcon 9 launches are scheduled for today, deploying Starlink satellites to enhance global internet coverage.
top_events.action: Watch the live streams for real-time updates.

top_events.title: Electron Launch for Earth Imaging
top_events.description: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket will launch the StriX satellite for Earth imaging, contributing to urban planning and disaster response.
top_events.action: Follow the launch for insights into satellite technology.

alerts.title: Geomagnetic Storm Alert
alerts.message: A geomagnetic storm with a Kp index of 6.33 is forecasted for March 19, offering a great chance to view auroras at latitudes as low as 55°.
alerts.action_required: Prepare for aurora viewing if you're in the visibility range.

opportunities.recommendation: Good viewing opportunity for high-latitude observers. Worth checking if you're above 60° latitude and skies are clear.
Possible aurora at high latitudes. Best for northern locations (65°+). Worth checking if conditions are optimal.
